surfaceComplaint: Full self-driving may be becoming real, but the downstream consequence map is sprawling.
promotedProblem: Vision-based autonomy becoming operationally and legally real removes or rewrites several separate substrates at once.
implications: Some downstream institutions lose the event stream that fed them.
Some markets reprice time, distance, ownership, and compliance around autonomous mobility.
The rollout lands unevenly, so boundaries between served and unserved worlds become part of the thesis.

possiblePepsis.name: Accident economy dries up
possiblePepsis.logic: Win by tracking what depends on the crash event stream disappearing.
possiblePepsis.rationale: Crash removal changes insurance, repair, legal, trauma, media, and hidden dependent systems.

possiblePepsis.name: Traffic-state loses its subject
possiblePepsis.logic: Win by tracking what happens when the human driver stops being a policeable and fineable subject.
possiblePepsis.rationale: Tickets, stops, searches, forfeiture, points, DUI workflows, and revenue replacement have a different substrate than crashes.

possiblePepsis.name: Ownership becomes fleet yield
possiblePepsis.logic: Win by tracking the private car changing from idle consumer asset to service/fleet node.
possiblePepsis.rationale: This rewrites dealers, auto credit, residual values, OEM model, repair, fuel, and parking through ownership economics.

possiblePepsis.name: Time and distance reprice
possiblePepsis.logic: Win by tracking door-to-door autonomy as a collapse in felt distance and active driving time.
possiblePepsis.rationale: This is one mobility/time story with chapters: travel substitution, labor/reclaimed time, and geography.

possiblePepsis.name: Adoption asymmetry
possiblePepsis.logic: Use uneven rollout as the governing lens across the other Pepsis.
possiblePepsis.rationale: This shapes where each Pepsi lands first; it is not just another industry branch.

possiblePepsis.name: Fleet compute becomes mobile edge
possiblePepsis.logic: Win by treating autonomous fleets as distributed sensing and compute infrastructure.
possiblePepsis.rationale: Interesting but less validated in this corpus; it needs a real demand router before it becomes a strong Pepsi.

tactics.name: Depth-map hidden dependents
tactics.howItPursues: Follow the missing crash event into dependents that are not obviously about cars.
hardConstraints: Requires the autonomy premise to reduce crash volume, not just shift liability.
softAxes: Visible first-order branch; value depends on second- and third-order depth.
Risk of double-counting with ownership and liability branches.

tactics.name: Map enforcement revenue replacement
tactics.howItPursues: Track the move from fines, stops, and fuel taxes toward movement metering and fleet/account compliance.
hardConstraints: Governments may claw back revenue instead of letting the stream disappear.
softAxes: Civil-liberties bargain may dominate the economics.

tactics.name: Game the ownership fork
tactics.howItPursues: Compare pure service fleets against owner-operated robotaxi yield and map who wins under each.
hardConstraints: Depends on utilization, regulation, insurance, and fleet operations actually clearing.
softAxes: May resolve differently by region and income segment.

tactics.name: Trace door-to-door substitution
tactics.howItPursues: Follow autonomous travel into aviation, commute time, labor markets, roadside economy, and geography.
hardConstraints: Only applies where autonomous service is reliable across the corridor.
softAxes: Rollout patchiness can delay or localize the effect.

checks.label: Pipes carry water
checks.detail: The board shows a multi-Pepsi case split by different logics of winning rather than one flat FSD cascade.

checks.label: Water not poisoned
checks.detail: The display distinguishes Pepsis, the adoption lens, candidates, and tactics instead of treating every branch as equal.

checks.label: Shower test
checks.detail: The multi-Pepsi board can still become dense; the display should remain scannable before the candidate wall.
